questions.prompt: The marketing team ______ the revised proposal before Friday's deadline.
questions.explanation: The phrase "before Friday's deadline" points to a future action, so the future form "will review" is correct. "Reviewing" and "to review" cannot serve as the main verb of the sentence.

questions.prompt: All visitors must ______ at the front desk before entering the laboratory.
questions.explanation: The modal verb "must" is always followed by the base form of a verb, so "sign in" is correct.

questions.prompt: Ms. Tran was promoted to regional manager because of ______ outstanding sales record.
questions.explanation: A possessive adjective is needed before the noun phrase "outstanding sales record." "Her" modifies the noun; "hers" stands alone and cannot be followed by a noun.

questions.prompt: The new printers are considerably faster ______ the models they replaced.
questions.explanation: Comparative adjectives such as "faster" pair with "than" to introduce the item being compared.

questions.prompt: ______ the heavy rain, the outdoor career fair proceeded as scheduled.
questions.explanation: A preposition is required before the noun phrase "the heavy rain." "Although" and "because" introduce full clauses, and "however" connects independent sentences.

questions.prompt: Employees who ______ to attend the training session should notify Human Resources by Monday.
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun "who" refers to the plural noun "employees," so the verb must be the plural present form "plan."

questions.prompt: The invoice must be paid ______ thirty days of receipt.
questions.explanation: "Within + a period of time" means before that period ends. "Within thirty days of receipt" is the standard business phrasing.

questions.prompt: Neither the manager nor the assistants ______ available to answer calls during the meeting.
questions.explanation: With "neither ... nor," the verb agrees with the subject closest to it. "Assistants" is plural, so "are" is correct.

questions.prompt: The committee will announce its decision ______ the review process is complete.
questions.explanation: A conjunction is needed to join the two clauses. "Once" means "as soon as." "During," "despite," and "among" are prepositions and cannot introduce a clause.

questions.prompt: Employees ______ parking permits expire this month should renew them online.
questions.explanation: A possessive relative pronoun is needed to show that the parking permits belong to the employees, so "whose" is correct.

questions.prompt: The leadership seminar has been postponed ______ next Thursday.
questions.explanation: "Postponed until" indicates the new time when the event will take place. "By" would set a deadline, which does not fit the meaning.

questions.prompt: Please review the attached report ______ and send me your comments by noon.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb "review," so the correct choice is "carefully."

questions.prompt: The store will ______ customers for any items damaged during delivery.
questions.explanation: "Reimburse" means to pay someone back for money lost or spent. A store replaces items, but it reimburses customers.

questions.prompt: Mr. Osei's flight was delayed, so he asked to ______ his hotel reservation by one night.
questions.explanation: "Extend a reservation" is the standard collocation for making a stay longer. "Expand" and "increase" describe size or quantity, not duration of a booking.

questions.prompt: The finance department reviews all travel ______ before issuing payments.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after "travel." "Expenses" is the noun form; the other choices are an adjective, a verb, and an adverb.

questions.prompt: Applicants must submit two letters of ______ along with their resume.
questions.explanation: "Letters of recommendation" is the fixed phrase for references submitted with a job application.

questions.prompt: The workshop is designed to help managers resolve workplace conflicts ______.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to describe how conflicts are resolved, so "effectively" is correct.

questions.prompt: Due to a scheduling ______, the product demonstration has been moved to Room 204.
questions.explanation: "Scheduling conflict" is the standard phrase for two events booked at the same time.

questions.prompt: Sales of home office furniture rose ______ during the first quarter.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb "rose." "Rose sharply" means increased quickly and by a large amount.

questions.prompt: The museum relies heavily on ______ from local businesses to fund its programs.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed as the object of "relies on." "Donations" is the plural noun meaning money or goods given to support an organization.

questions.prompt: By the time the auditors arrive, the accounting team ______ the quarterly reports.
questions.explanation: "By the time + present tense" describes a point in the future, so the main clause takes the future perfect "will have completed."

questions.prompt: The keynote speech was ______ received by conference attendees.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb "received." "Warmly received" is the standard collocation.

questions.prompt: Employees may park in the visitor lot ______ they display a valid permit.
questions.explanation: A conjunction is needed to introduce the condition clause. "Provided that" means "on the condition that." The other choices are prepositions or infinitive markers.

questions.prompt: ______ of the two proposals offers a realistic timeline for the renovation.
questions.explanation: The singular verb "offers" and the phrase "of the two" require "neither," which is used for two items and takes a singular verb. "None" is used for three or more, and "both" would need a plural verb.

questions.prompt: The seminar registration fee is fully refundable ______ cancellation is received five days in advance.
questions.explanation: The sentence needs a conditional conjunction: the fee is refundable on the condition that cancellation arrives early. "Unless" would reverse the meaning.

questions.prompt: Sales representatives are encouraged to respond to customer inquiries as ______ as possible.
questions.explanation: The structure "as ... as possible" modifies the verb "respond," so an adverb is required: "as promptly as possible."

questions.prompt: The company's new headquarters is conveniently ______ near the central train station.
questions.explanation: The passive construction "is located" describes where a building stands. "Conveniently located" is the standard phrase.

questions.prompt: ______ joining Hartwell Consulting, Ms. Alvarez worked as a financial analyst in Chicago.
questions.explanation: The past tense "worked" describes her job prior to Hartwell, so "Before joining" is correct. "Since joining" would require the present perfect.

questions.prompt: Please direct any questions ______ the upcoming merger to the communications office.
questions.explanation: "Regarding" is a preposition meaning "about" and correctly links "questions" to their topic.

questions.prompt: The maintenance crew will inspect the ventilation system ______ the building is closed for the holiday.
questions.explanation: A conjunction is needed before the clause "the building is closed." "During" is a preposition and must be followed by a noun, not a clause.

questions.prompt: By the time the auditors arrive next week, the finance team ______ the quarterly reports.
questions.explanation: "By the time" plus a future reference point calls for the future perfect, which describes an action finished before that point: "will have completed."

questions.prompt: Mr. Okada ______ for Meridian Logistics since it opened its Osaka branch in 2019.
questions.explanation: "Since 2019" marks an action that began in the past and continues now, so the present perfect "has worked" is correct.

questions.prompt: While the technicians ______ the server, employees were asked to save their files.
questions.explanation: The past continuous "were upgrading" describes an action in progress when another past action ("were asked") occurred.

questions.prompt: The shipment ______ at the warehouse before the manager checked the inventory.
questions.explanation: The past perfect "had arrived" shows that the shipment's arrival happened before another past action, "checked."

questions.prompt: Starting next month, the cafeteria ______ breakfast from 7:00 to 9:30 a.m.
questions.explanation: "Starting next month" points to the future, so the future form "will serve" is required.

questions.prompt: When Ms. Alvarez called, the client ______ another meeting.
questions.explanation: An action in progress at a past moment ("When Ms. Alvarez called") takes the past continuous: "was attending."

questions.prompt: The company ______ three new distribution centers since January.
questions.explanation: "Since January" connects a past starting point to the present, which requires the present perfect "has opened."

questions.prompt: The committee recommended ______ the product launch until the packaging issue is resolved.
questions.explanation: The verb "recommend" is followed by a gerund, so "postponing" is correct.

questions.prompt: Employees are encouraged ______ any safety concerns to their supervisor.
questions.explanation: The passive pattern "be encouraged" takes an infinitive: "are encouraged to report."

questions.prompt: Before ______ the contract, please review the payment terms carefully.
questions.explanation: After the preposition "before," a verb takes the gerund form: "before signing."

questions.prompt: The new software allows users ______ reports in several formats.
questions.explanation: The pattern "allow someone to do something" requires the infinitive "to export."

questions.prompt: Ms. Patel suggested ______ the workshop to accommodate more attendees.
questions.explanation: "Suggest" is followed by a gerund, so "relocating" is correct; "suggest to relocate" is not a valid pattern.

questions.prompt: All applicants must be willing ______ occasional weekend shifts.
questions.explanation: The adjective "willing" is followed by an infinitive: "willing to work."

questions.prompt: The instructions ______ to the package explain how to assemble the shelf.
questions.explanation: A past participle is needed to modify "instructions" with a passive meaning: the instructions are attached to the package, so "attached" is correct.

questions.prompt: Avoid ______ the machine while the maintenance light is on.
questions.explanation: "Avoid" is followed by a gerund, so "operating" is correct.

questions.prompt: Employees who lose ______ badges should contact the security office immediately.
questions.explanation: A possessive adjective is needed before the noun "badges," so "their" is correct; "theirs" cannot be followed by a noun.

questions.prompt: The design proposal from Ardent Studio was more detailed than ______.
questions.explanation: The possessive pronoun "ours" stands in for "our proposal," completing the comparison without repeating the noun.

questions.prompt: Mr. Chen prepared the entire presentation ______, without any help from his team.
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun "himself" emphasizes that Mr. Chen did the work alone.

questions.prompt: Participants may help ______ to refreshments in the lobby during the break.
questions.explanation: The idiom "help oneself to" requires a reflexive pronoun that refers back to the subject: "help themselves."

questions.prompt: The office manager asked Julia and ______ to take inventory of the supply room.
questions.explanation: The pronoun is an object of the verb "asked," so the object form "me" is correct: "asked Julia and me."

questions.prompt: The clients requested that the samples be sent directly to ______.
questions.explanation: After the preposition "to," the object pronoun "them" is required.

questions.prompt: The marketing department exceeded ______ quarterly target by a wide margin.
questions.explanation: A possessive adjective is needed before "quarterly target." "Its" is possessive; "it's" is a contraction of "it is."

questions.prompt: Each of the interns received a mentor who will guide ______ through the first month.
questions.explanation: The verb "guide" needs an object pronoun referring to the interns, so "them" is correct.

questions.prompt: Ms. Ito's proposal and ______ were both approved by the board.
questions.explanation: The possessive pronoun "mine" replaces "my proposal" and functions as part of the subject.

questions.prompt: The new express service is significantly ______ than standard shipping.
questions.explanation: "Than" signals a comparison between two things, so the comparative form "faster" is required.

questions.prompt: Of all the venues we visited, the Harborview Center was the ______.
questions.explanation: "Of all the venues" compares more than two items, so the superlative "the most spacious" is correct.

questions.prompt: Sales in March were twice as ______ as in February.
questions.explanation: The equative pattern "as ... as" takes the base form of the adjective: "twice as high as."

questions.prompt: The ______ we submit the proposal, the sooner we will receive feedback.
questions.explanation: The parallel pattern "the + comparative ..., the + comparative ..." requires "earlier" to match "the sooner."

questions.prompt: This model consumes far ______ energy than its predecessor.
questions.explanation: "Energy" is uncountable and the sentence compares two models, so the comparative "less" is correct.

questions.prompt: No other branch processes orders as ______ as the Denver office.
questions.explanation: The "as ... as" pattern takes the base form, and an adverb is needed to modify "processes": "as efficiently as."

questions.prompt: The updated handbook is much more ______ than the previous edition.
questions.explanation: After "more ... than," an adjective is needed to describe the handbook: "more comprehensive."

questions.prompt: Among the three bids, Kestrel Construction's was the ______ expensive.
questions.explanation: Comparing three bids requires the superlative "the least expensive"; "less" only compares two.

questions.prompt: This is the ______ single order the factory has received this year.
questions.explanation: The phrase "the ... this year" ranks the order against all others, so the superlative "largest" is correct.

questions.prompt: The invoice must be paid ______ thirty days of receipt.
questions.explanation: "Within" expresses a deadline inside a period of time: "within thirty days of receipt."

questions.prompt: Ms. Danvers will be out of the office ______ Monday, when she returns from the trade fair.
questions.explanation: "Until" marks the point in time when the situation ends: she is away up to Monday.

questions.prompt: The conference center is conveniently located ______ the airport and the harbor.
questions.explanation: "Between" is used for two reference points (the airport and the harbor); "among" needs three or more.

questions.prompt: ______ inclement weather, the outdoor reception will be moved to the atrium.
questions.explanation: "In the event of" means "if something happens" and fits the contingency plan for bad weather.

questions.prompt: The seminar was rescheduled ______ a conflict with the annual sales meeting.
questions.explanation: A preposition is needed before the noun phrase "a conflict." "Due to" takes a noun; "because" and "since" introduce full clauses.

questions.prompt: The list of approved vendors ______ updated every quarter.
questions.explanation: The subject is the singular noun "list," not "vendors," so the singular verb "is" is correct.

questions.prompt: Neither the printer nor the copiers ______ working this morning.
questions.explanation: With "neither ... nor," the verb agrees with the nearer subject, the plural "copiers," so "are" is correct.

questions.prompt: Each of the branch offices ______ its own training coordinator.
questions.explanation: "Each" is grammatically singular even when followed by "of the branch offices," so the singular "has" is correct.

questions.prompt: The number of online orders ______ doubled since the website redesign.
questions.explanation: "The number of" takes a singular verb, so "has doubled" is correct; "a number of" would take a plural verb.

questions.prompt: Everyone attending the workshop ______ asked to bring a laptop.
questions.explanation: Indefinite pronouns like "everyone" are singular, so the singular verb "is" is correct.

questions.prompt: The results of the customer survey ______ expected later this week.
questions.explanation: The plural subject "results" requires the plural verb "are"; "of the customer survey" is only a modifier.

questions.prompt: Economics ______ among the most popular electives at the training institute.
questions.explanation: Subject names ending in "-ics," such as "economics," are treated as singular, so "is" is correct.

questions.prompt: ______ the flight was delayed by two hours, Ms. Park arrived in time for the keynote address.
questions.explanation: A conjunction is needed to introduce the clause "the flight was delayed." "Despite" and "because of" are prepositions, and "however" cannot join two clauses this way.

questions.prompt: Please silence your mobile phone ______ the performance is in progress.
questions.explanation: "While" introduces the clause "the performance is in progress." "During" is a preposition and must be followed by a noun, not a clause.

questions.prompt: The order will ship today ______ the payment is confirmed by 2 p.m.
questions.explanation: "Provided that" introduces the condition under which the order ships; the other choices cannot introduce a conditional clause.

questions.prompt: Conference attendees may pay the registration fee by credit card ______ by bank transfer.
questions.explanation: "Or" joins the two alternative payment methods; "nor" requires a preceding negative such as "neither."

questions.prompt: ______ you have any questions about your benefits, please contact Human Resources.
questions.explanation: "If" introduces the conditional clause about having questions; the other words do not fit the sentence structure.

questions.prompt: The awards banquet was moved to the ballroom ______ more guests could be accommodated.
questions.explanation: "So that" introduces a purpose clause with its own subject and verb ("more guests could be accommodated"); "in order to" must be followed by a base verb.

questions.prompt: Applicants ______ résumés are received by Friday will be contacted for interviews next week.
questions.explanation: The possessive relative pronoun "whose" links "applicants" to "résumés": the résumés belong to the applicants.

questions.prompt: The consultant ______ the committee hired last month specializes in supply-chain logistics.
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun is the object of "hired," so the object form "whom" is correct; "which" cannot refer to a person.

questions.prompt: The leadership workshop, ______ was originally scheduled for May, has been moved to June.
questions.explanation: A nonrestrictive clause set off by commas takes "which"; "that" cannot follow a comma in this pattern.

questions.prompt: Employees ______ park in the visitor lot will be asked to move their cars.
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun is the subject of "park" and refers to people, so "who" is correct.

questions.prompt: The report summarizes the factors ______ contributed to the shipping delay.
questions.explanation: "That" is the subject of "contributed" and refers to the things ("factors"); "who" refers only to people.

questions.prompt: The hotel at ______ the delegates stayed offers a complimentary airport shuttle.
questions.explanation: After a preposition, only "which" can be used for a thing: "at which the delegates stayed." "That" and "where" cannot follow "at" here.

questions.prompt: The orientation covers everything ______ new hires need to know about payroll and benefits.
questions.explanation: "That" links "everything" to the clause; "what" cannot follow a noun it would describe because it already means "the thing that."

questions.prompt: The innovation award will go to ______ submits the most original design concept.
questions.explanation: "Whoever" is the subject of "submits," so the subject form is required even though the clause follows the preposition "to."

questions.prompt: The building ______ the agency occupies was completely renovated last year.
questions.explanation: The verb "occupies" needs an object, so the relative pronoun "that" is correct; "where" cannot serve as an object.

questions.prompt: The maintenance crew responded ______ to the reported water leak.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb "responded," so "promptly" is correct.

questions.prompt: The two laboratories operate ______ of each other.
questions.explanation: An adverb is required to modify the verb "operate": "operate independently."

questions.prompt: Ticket prices have risen ______ since the stadium renovation was announced.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to describe how prices have risen, so "sharply" is correct.

questions.prompt: The CEO's remarks about the merger were ______ received by investors.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb "were received," so "favorably" is correct.

questions.prompt: ______, the shipment arrived a full day ahead of schedule.
questions.explanation: A sentence adverb is needed to comment on the whole statement, so "Surprisingly" is correct.

questions.prompt: The museum offers a 10 percent ______ to members of partner organizations.
questions.explanation: A "discount" is a reduction in price offered to certain customers. A "refund" returns money after a purchase, which does not fit here.

questions.prompt: Please ______ your seat assignment before boarding the express train.
questions.explanation: "Confirm" means to check that something is correct or final, which fits a seat assignment; the other verbs take different kinds of objects.

questions.prompt: The city council approved additional funds to ______ the aging pedestrian bridge.
questions.explanation: "Repair" means to fix something damaged or worn, which fits an aging bridge; "revise" applies to documents and plans.

questions.prompt: Employees traveling for business may request a daily ______ to cover meals and lodging.
questions.explanation: An "allowance" is a fixed amount of money given for expenses; "permission" and "admission" are not sums of money.

questions.prompt: The recall affects only a small ______ of the devices sold last year.
questions.explanation: "Portion" means a part of a whole, so "a small portion of the devices" is correct; "sector" refers to an area of an economy or industry.

questions.prompt: The two engineering firms have agreed to ______ their research divisions next year.
questions.explanation: "Merge" means to combine two organizations into one, which fits the two research divisions; "expand" takes a single thing being enlarged.

questions.prompt: The selection committee expects to ______ a decision on the vendor contract by Friday.
questions.explanation: "Reach a decision" is the standard collocation; "arrive" and "come" would need "at," and "achieve a decision" is not idiomatic.

questions.prompt: Please ______ close attention to the revised evacuation procedures.
questions.explanation: "Pay attention" is the fixed collocation; the other verbs do not combine with "attention."

questions.prompt: The keynote speaker will ______ a speech on emerging Asian markets.
questions.explanation: "Deliver a speech" is the standard collocation; "say" and "talk" do not take "a speech" as an object this way.

questions.prompt: The IT department ______ every effort to restore email service within an hour.
questions.explanation: "Make an effort" is the fixed collocation, so the past form "made every effort" is correct.

questions.prompt: All new employees are required to ______ part in the two-day orientation session.
questions.explanation: "Take part in" is the fixed expression meaning to participate.

questions.prompt: The ______ of the new inventory policy will begin at all stores next month.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed as the subject of the sentence, so "implementation" is correct.

questions.prompt: Her ______ to detail makes her an excellent proofreader.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the possessive "her," so "attention" is correct: "attention to detail."

questions.prompt: The Grandview Hotel underwent a complete ______ last spring.
questions.explanation: An article and adjective ("a complete") must be followed by a noun, so "renovation" is correct.

questions.prompt: Please direct any ______ about billing to the accounts receivable team.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after "any," so the plural noun "inquiries" is correct.

questions.prompt: The ______ between the two departments improved noticeably after the joint retreat.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed as the subject of "improved," so "collaboration" is correct.

questions.prompt: Growing ______ for organic produce has reshaped the supermarket's layout.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed as the head of the subject phrase, so "demand" is correct: "growing demand for organic produce."

questions.prompt: The safety inspector noted several ______ of the fire code in the stairwell.
questions.explanation: After "several," a plural noun is required, so "violations" is correct.

questions.prompt: ______ to the fitness center is included in the monthly membership fee.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed as the subject of "is included," so "Access" is correct.

questions.prompt: ______ of the conference rooms is equipped with a ceiling-mounted projector.
questions.explanation: The singular verb "is" requires the singular quantifier "Each"; "All," "Most," and "Both" would take "are."

questions.prompt: There are only a ______ tickets left for the awards banquet.
questions.explanation: "Tickets" is a countable plural noun, so "a few" is correct; "a little" and "much" are used with uncountable nouns.

questions.prompt: ______ information about the merger has been released to the press so far.
questions.explanation: "Information" is uncountable, so "little" is correct; "few" and "many" are used with countable nouns.

questions.prompt: The company received ______ applications than expected for the summer internship.
questions.explanation: "Applications" is countable and the sentence compares with "than," so the comparative "fewer" is correct; "less" is for uncountable nouns.

questions.prompt: ______ employees have already signed up for the wellness program this year.
questions.explanation: "Several" fits the plural countable noun "employees"; "much" and "little" are for uncountable nouns, and "every" takes a singular noun.

questions.prompt: Please take as ______ brochures as you need for your clients.
questions.explanation: "Brochures" is countable, so "as many ... as" is correct; "as much" is used with uncountable nouns.

questions.prompt: ______ of the feedback we received about the new menu was positive.
questions.explanation: "Feedback" is uncountable, so "Most" is correct; "many," "few," and "several" require countable plural nouns.

questions.prompt: The parking garage has ______ spaces available on weekends.
questions.explanation: "Plenty of" works with plural countable nouns like "spaces"; "much," "a little," and "a great deal of" are for uncountable nouns.

questions.prompt: ______ candidate must submit two professional references with the application.
questions.explanation: The singular noun "candidate" requires "Every"; "all," "some," and "many" take plural nouns.

questions.prompt: The warehouse supervisor will ______ the delivery schedule after the supplier confirms the shipping date.
questions.explanation: After the modal "will," use the base verb "revise."

questions.prompt: Employees must ______ their identification badges while working in the production area.
questions.explanation: The modal "must" is followed by the base verb "wear."

questions.prompt: The training video was ______ by the compliance department last month.
questions.explanation: The passive structure "was produced" is needed because the video receives the action.

questions.prompt: Please ______ the attached form before sending it to payroll.
questions.explanation: The imperative sentence uses the base verb "complete."

questions.prompt: The board has ______ the proposal for a second distribution center.
questions.explanation: The present perfect uses "has" plus the past participle "approved."

questions.prompt: The accounting team ______ the annual budget report yesterday afternoon.
questions.explanation: The time expression "yesterday afternoon" requires the simple past verb "finalized."

questions.prompt: By the time the clients arrive, the reception staff ______ the conference room.
questions.explanation: "By the time" plus a future reference calls for the future perfect to show completion before arrival.

questions.prompt: The company ______ its online catalog twice since January.
questions.explanation: "Since January" connects a past starting point to the present, so the present perfect is correct.

questions.prompt: The maintenance crew ______ the damaged sign when the store manager called.
questions.explanation: The past progressive describes an action in progress when another past event happened.

questions.prompt: Next quarter, the sales department ______ a new customer survey.
questions.explanation: "Next quarter" refers to the future, so "will launch" fits.

questions.prompt: The revised employee handbook will be available ______ the company intranet.
questions.explanation: Information is available "on" an intranet or website.

questions.prompt: All invoices must be submitted ______ ten business days of travel.
questions.explanation: "Within ten business days" means before that period ends.

questions.prompt: The seminar is open ______ managers from every regional branch.
questions.explanation: The phrase is "open to" a group of people.

questions.prompt: The replacement parts are stored ______ the locked cabinet near the loading dock.
questions.explanation: Items are stored "in" a cabinet.

questions.prompt: The consultant spoke ______ the importance of clear product descriptions.
questions.explanation: The verb "spoke" is commonly followed by "about" to introduce a topic.

questions.prompt: The manager praised Lena for handling the customer complaint ______.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify "handling," so "professionally" is correct.

questions.prompt: The new software allows technicians to update service records more ______.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed after "more" to modify the verb "update."

questions.prompt: The delivery driver checked the address ______ before leaving the warehouse.
questions.explanation: The adverb "carefully" modifies the verb "checked."

questions.prompt: The marketing report was written ______ enough for new employees to understand.
questions.explanation: The adverb "clearly" modifies "was written."

questions.prompt: All safety inspections must be documented ______.
questions.explanation: The adverb "thoroughly" describes how inspections must be documented.

questions.prompt: The hotel received many positive comments about the ______ of its front-desk staff.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after "the" and before the prepositional phrase, so "courtesy" is correct.

questions.prompt: The purchasing department requested a written ______ before approving the repair work.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the adjective "written," and "estimate" fits the meaning.

questions.prompt: The factory tour begins with a brief ______ of safety procedures.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after "a brief," so "explanation" is correct.

questions.prompt: The finance director asked for ______ of the travel expenses.
questions.explanation: A noun is required after the preposition "for."

questions.prompt: The ______ of the new branch office attracted several local officials.
questions.explanation: The noun "opening" refers to the event of starting operations.

questions.prompt: The customer service team gave a ______ response to every inquiry.
questions.explanation: An adjective is needed to modify the noun "response."

questions.prompt: The warehouse manager chose a ______ route for the delivery trucks.
questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before the noun "route."

questions.prompt: The conference organizer reserved a ______ room for the keynote speaker.
questions.explanation: The adjective "private" modifies the noun "room."

questions.prompt: The instructions on the warranty form were ______ to most customers.
questions.explanation: The adjective "confusing" describes the instructions.

questions.prompt: The updated brochure includes a ______ map of the convention center.
questions.explanation: The adjective "detailed" modifies "map."

questions.prompt: The venue is ______ than the hotel ballroom used last year.
questions.explanation: The comparison with "than" requires the comparative form "more spacious."

questions.prompt: This quarter's revenue was ______ than analysts had predicted.
questions.explanation: The word "than" signals a comparative adjective.

questions.prompt: Of the three proposals, Ms. Ivers submitted the ______ cost estimate.
questions.explanation: "Of the three" calls for the superlative form "lowest."

questions.prompt: The new checkout process is ______ efficient than the previous one.
questions.explanation: For a multi-syllable adjective, use "more efficient" for the comparative.

questions.prompt: The downtown branch is the ______ location in the region.
questions.explanation: The phrase "the ... in the region" requires a superlative adjective.

questions.prompt: The shipment was delayed ______ the loading dock was closed for repairs.
questions.explanation: "Because" introduces the reason for the delay.

questions.prompt: Please review the contract carefully ______ signing it.

Administrative Assistant — Kelton Medical Group.
Kelton Medical Group seeks an administrative assistant for its Northside clinic. Duties include greeting patients, updating appointment records, preparing insurance forms, and ordering office supplies.
Applicants should have at least one year of office experience and strong organizational skills. Experience with medical billing software is preferred but not required.
The position is full time, Monday through Friday. Send a resume to jobs@keltonmedical.com by June 12.

To: Rachel Bell. From: Omar Haddad, Event Coordinator. Subject: Northgate Marketing Summit. Date: October 18.
Dear Ms. Bell,
Thank you for agreeing to lead a session at this year's Northgate Marketing Summit. You are currently scheduled for the 2:00 P.M. slot in Salon C on Friday, November 8. Please confirm your participation by October 25.
As a presenter, you will receive complimentary registration for the full summit. Badges can be collected at the speaker desk in the main lobby.
You asked about equipment: every salon is equipped with a projector, but laptops are not provided, so please bring your own.
Best regards,
Omar Haddad

Northgate Marketing Summit — Friday, November 8. Session schedule.
10:00 A.M. — Salon A — Social Media Trends — D. Okafor.
11:00 A.M. — Salon B — E-mail Campaigns That Work — L. Chen.
2:00 P.M. — Salon C — Measuring Brand Loyalty — R. Bell.
3:30 P.M. — Salon A — Data-Driven Design — M. Fontaine.
Update (November 1): Because of an extended lunch program, all Salon C sessions will begin 30 minutes later than shown.

questions.explanation: The e-mail explains that salons have projectors but laptops are not provided, so presenters should bring their own.

SwiftClean Pro VX200 cordless vacuum.
Powerful cleaning with a 60-minute battery, three snap-on attachments, and a two-year warranty.
Special offer: order by January 31 and receive a free replacement filter set.
Free shipping on all orders over $200.
Price: $249. Order online at swiftcleanpro.com.

Customer review — posted February 4 by J. Morita. Rating: 4 out of 5 stars.
I ordered my VX200 on January 28, and it arrived in just three days with no shipping charge.
The battery genuinely lasts the full hour, and the vacuum is light enough to carry upstairs.
I was disappointed that only two attachments were in the box. When I called customer service, I waited twenty minutes to speak with someone, but they mailed the missing attachment the same day.
Overall, I would recommend this vacuum.

questions.prompt: According to the advertisement, what is included with the VX200?
questions.explanation: The advertisement lists three snap-on attachments, along with a 60-minute battery and a two-year warranty.

questions.prompt: Why did Mr. Morita's order qualify for free shipping?

options.A: It was placed during a holiday sale

questions.explanation: The advertisement offers free shipping on orders over $200, and the vacuum costs $249.

questions.prompt: What can be concluded about Mr. Morita's order?

options.A: It qualified for the free filter set
options.C: It was placed at a retail store

questions.explanation: The offer applies to orders placed by January 31, and the review says he ordered on January 28, so his order qualified for the free replacement filter set.

questions.prompt: What problem does Mr. Morita mention in his review?

options.B: The vacuum was too heavy to carry
options.C: An attachment was missing from the box

questions.explanation: The review says only two of the three attachments were in the box, so one was missing.

questions.prompt: What does Mr. Morita indicate about customer service?

options.A: It was slow to answer but resolved his problem quickly
options.B: It refused to replace the missing part
options.C: It could not be reached by telephone
options.D: It offered him a full refund

questions.explanation: He waited twenty minutes to speak with someone, but the missing attachment was mailed the same day.

Corner Bean Coffee Roasters — wholesale program.
Freshly roasted beans delivered to your cafe or office.
Wholesale prices per 5-pound bag: House Blend $52. Dark Roast $55. Single-Origin Peru $68.
Orders of ten bags or more receive a 10 percent discount.
Standard delivery takes three business days; next-day delivery is available for $15 per order.
New wholesale customers receive a complimentary tasting kit with their first order.

Corner Bean Coffee Roasters — Order #8802.
Customer: Solstice Cafe (first-time wholesale customer). Order date: Tuesday, April 8.
8 x House Blend, 5-pound bag — $416.
4 x Single-Origin Peru, 5-pound bag — $272.
Subtotal: $688. Discount (10 percent): -$68.80. Next-day delivery: $15.
Total: $634.20.
Expected delivery: Wednesday, April 9.

To: orders@cornerbean.com. From: Talia Brooks, Solstice Cafe. Date: April 10. Subject: Order #8802.
Hello,
The beans arrived on schedule yesterday, and the quality is excellent.
However, the complimentary tasting kit mentioned in your advertisement was not included in the shipment. Could you send it with our next order?
Also, please change our standing order to twelve bags of House Blend beginning in May.
Thank you.

Brightwater Institute — professional development workshops, spring series.
All workshops run 9:00 A.M. to 4:00 P.M. at our downtown campus and include lunch.
April 15 — Negotiation Essentials ($180) — Room 210.
April 22 — Project Budgeting ($195) — Room 108.
May 6 — Presenting with Confidence ($180) — Auditorium.
Register at brightwater.org/workshops. Participants who register for two or more workshops receive 15 percent off the total.
Cancellations made at least seven days in advance receive a full refund.

Brightwater Institute — registration confirmation.
Name: Victor Salazar, Meridian Logistics. Date issued: March 30.
Workshops: Project Budgeting (April 22); Presenting with Confidence (May 6).
Total before discount: $375. Discount applied: 15 percent. Amount charged: $318.75.
Note: please bring a laptop to Project Budgeting.

To: registrations@brightwater.org. From: v.salazar@meridianlog.com. Date: May 1. Subject: May 6 workshop.
Hello,
I attended Project Budgeting last week and found it very practical.
Unfortunately, I have been assigned to a client visit on May 6 and can no longer attend the second workshop. I understand the refund deadline has passed, so instead of canceling, could my colleague Dana Ferro attend in my place?
She is vegetarian, if that matters for the lunch arrangements.
Thank you.

questions.explanation: The announcement states that all workshops run from 9:00 A.M. to 4:00 P.M. and include lunch.

Westport Civic Center — room rental rates.
Harbor Hall seats 180 people and costs $640 for a full day. Room B seats 75 people and costs $310 for a full day. Room C seats 40 people and costs $190 for a full day.
Audio equipment is included in Harbor Hall only. Projectors may be rented for $35 per room.
Catering setup requires a separate $60 cleaning fee. Reservations must be confirmed with a 25 percent deposit.

To: rentals@westportcivic.org. From: Martin Hale, Ardent Training. Date: June 4. Subject: July 18 seminar.
Hello,
We expect about 65 participants for our customer-service seminar on July 18. We will need a projector, but we are not arranging food service.
Room B appears to be the best fit. Please hold it for us and send an invoice for the required deposit.
Thank you,
Martin Hale

LumaDesk task lamp — model LD-40.
The LD-40 has three brightness settings, a flexible arm, and an automatic shutoff after four hours. The lamp includes a one-year warranty covering defects in materials and workmanship.
Replacement bulbs are sold separately for $8 each. Customers may return unused lamps within 30 days with a receipt.

To: service@lumadesk.com. From: Amira Patel. Date: November 12. Subject: LD-40 lamp.
Hello,
I bought an LD-40 lamp on October 20. I like the design, but yesterday the flexible arm stopped holding its position. I still have the receipt, and the lamp has been used only at my office desk.
Please let me know whether this can be repaired or replaced under the warranty.
Regards,
Amira Patel

To: Amira Patel. From: LumaDesk Customer Service. Date: November 13.
Dear Ms. Patel,
The problem you described appears to be covered by the product warranty. Please bring the lamp and your receipt to any authorized retailer, or mail the lamp to our service center using the prepaid label attached to this message.
Replacement bulbs are not required for this repair.

questions.prompt: What feature of the lamp stopped working properly?
questions.explanation: Ms. Patel writes that the flexible arm stopped holding its position.

questions.prompt: Why is Ms. Patel probably eligible for warranty service?

options.C: The purchase was made less than one year ago
options.D: The lamp has three brightness settings

questions.explanation: The warranty lasts one year, and Ms. Patel bought the lamp on October 20, less than a month before her message.

questions.prompt: What does the reply indicate about the problem?

options.A: It is likely covered by the warranty
options.B: It was caused by an incorrect bulb
options.C: It requires a paid replacement part

questions.explanation: Customer Service says the problem appears to be covered by the product warranty.

questions.prompt: What is one option Ms. Patel is given?

options.A: Return the lamp without a receipt
options.B: Bring the lamp to an authorized retailer
options.D: Wait for a technician to visit her office

questions.explanation: The reply says she may bring the lamp and receipt to any authorized retailer or mail it to the service center.

questions.prompt: What is attached to the customer service reply?
questions.explanation: The reply mentions a prepaid label attached to the message for mailing the lamp to the service center.
